nameMeanings.name: YR
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Hong, Jian, Qing, Feng, Zhong, Bin, Jin, Li, Mei, Min, Ming, Wen.  Korean Chol, Dong Sup, Jong Chul, Myong, Neng, Sook Young, Sung Chul.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 葉, meaning ‘leaf’ in Chinese: (i) from the placename Ye (葉), the name of a fief (located in Henan province) granted to Shen Zhu Liang, son of an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) traced back to a minority ethnic group in ancient southern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 野: shortened form of the Chinese surname Dong-Ye (東野), which was derived from name of a fief (located in Shandong province) granted to Yu, nephew of King Wu of Zhou (c. 1087–1043 BC ).

variants.name: Yip
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wai, Wing, Ching, Lai, Chi, Chi Hung, Chun, Kam, Kan, Man, Shu, Yuk.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 葉, see   Ye   1.

variants.name: Yep
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Jing, Shin, Tong, Wai, Wing Fong.
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surname 葉, see   Ye   1.

variants.name: Yap
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Enrique, Jose, Alberto, Carlos, Fernando, Rodolfo, Rolando, Rosalina, Vincente, Adela, Adoracion, Alfredo.

Chinese: Hakka and Hokkien Romanization of the surname 葉, see   Ye   1. The dialects using this form are found in Guangdong, Fujian and Taiwan, from where many people migrated to Singapore, Malaysia, the Philippines, and Thailand, and other parts of Southeast Asia.
Americanized form of North German   Japp  .

variants.name: Yu
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Ming, Yong, Young, Ping, Chi, Chun, Jae, Li, Sung, Jin, Yang.  Korean Chung, Chang, Byung, Chong, Min, Pyong, Myong, Hyong, Moon, Yiu, Inyoung, Shiu.

Chinese: Mandarin and Cantonese form of the surname 余 (or rarely written as 餘), meaning ‘I’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the second element of the personal name You Yu (由余), the name of a prime minister in the state of Qin during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from Yu (余), the name of a small state during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (iii) said to be borne by descendants of Xia Yu, the first king of the Xia dynasty (2070–1600  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin and Cantonese form of the surname 于 (meaning ‘go’ in ancient Chinese) or 於 (often simplified as 于 in Mainland China, meaning ‘depend on’ in ancient Chinese): (i) simplified form of the Chinese character 邘, the name of an ancient state (located in Qinyang in Henan province), which was granted to Yu Shu (邘叔), the third son of King Wen of Zhou (1152–1056 BC ). After it was annexed by the state of Zheng during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ), the Chinese character 邘 (pronounced Yu) was simplified to (于) and adopted as a surname. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Wan Niu Yu (萬忸于) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534 AD ). (iii) adopted in place of the Chinese compound surname Chun-Yu (淳于), because the first element of the surname, Chun, happened to share the same pronunciation with the personal name of the emperor Li Chun, also known as Emperor Xianzong of Tang (778–820 AD ), which was taboo in ancient China. (iv) said to be traced back to Yu Ze (於則), who said to be the inventor of shoes, an official the legendary Huang Di, the ‘Yellow Emperor’ (c. 27th century BC ). (v) from the second element of the placename Shang Yu (商於) in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ).

variants.name: Yeh
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Ching, Ming, Cheng, Chin, Hui, Hui Chun, Ping, Yuan, Chi, Chien, Ching Ling, Ching Yu.
Chinese:
alternative Mandarin form of the surname 葉, see   Ye   1.

nameMeanings.name: Meredydd
meanings: This is said to be the same name as Meriadek or Meiradwg, and also as the] Irish name O'Muireadwg, and the Scotch Mac Muireadaigh and Muir- eadhach. O'Reilly, however, renders the Irish word muireadach 'a sovereign, a lord j also a man's name, Morogh.' It means rather l admiral,' literally protector of the sea; from muir the sea, eadhach protector. I derive the British name from mawreddwg, mawreddig, magnificent, great, from mawr, great.

nameMeanings.name: AP
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of any of the names beginning with this syllable.

variants.name: Alexander
meanings: From the Latin form of the Greek name Alexandros, from alexein ‘to defend’ + anēr ‘man, warrior’ (genitive andros). The name became extremely popular in the post-classical period, and was borne by several individuals in the New Testament and some early Christian saints. Its use as a common given name throughout Europe, however, derives largely from the fame of Alexander the Great , King of Macedon ( 356–323 bc ), around whom a large body of popular legend grew up in late antiquity, much of which came to be embodied in the medieval ‘Alexander romances’.

variants.name: Alan
meanings: Of Celtic origin and uncertain derivation (possibly a diminutive of a word meaning ‘rock’). It was introduced into England by Breton followers of William the Conqueror , most notably Alan , Earl of Brittany, who was rewarded for his services with vast estates in the newly conquered kingdom. In Britain the variants Allan and Allen are considerably less frequent, and generally represent transferred uses of surname forms, whereas in America all three forms of the name are approximately equally common. See also Alun .

variants.name: Albert
meanings: From an Old French name, Albert, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from adal ‘noble’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to England, displacing the Old English form Æþelbeorht. The name is popular in a variety of forms in Western Europe, and has been traditional in a number of European princely families. It was out of favour in England for centuries, however, and the revival of its popularity in the 19th century was largely in honour of Queen Victoria's consort, Prince Albert of Saxe-Coburg-Gotha .

en.lifeSummary: Meredydd AP YR Arglwydd was born about 1182, in Machen, Monmouthshire, Wales as the son of Yr Arglwydd Ap Rhys. He had at least 1 son with Gwerfil Ap Morgan.

fr.lifeSummary: Meredydd AP YR Arglwydd est né environ 1182, à Machen, Monmouthshire, Galles, fils de Yr Arglwydd Ap Rhys. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Gwerfil Ap Morgan.

de.lifeSummary: Meredydd AP YR Arglwydd wurde um 1182 in Machen, Monmouthshire, Wales, als Sohn von Yr Arglwydd Ap Rhys geboren. Er hatte mit Gwerfil Ap Morgan mindestens 1 Sohn.

it.lifeSummary: Meredydd AP YR Arglwydd è nato il circa 1182 nel Machen, Monmouthshire, Galles come figlio di Yr Arglwydd Ap Rhys. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio con Gwerfil Ap Morgan.

pt.lifeSummary: Meredydd AP YR Arglwydd nasceu aproximadamente 1182, em Machen, Monmouthshire, Gales filho de Yr Arglwydd Ap Rhys. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho com Gwerfil Ap Morgan.

ru.lifeSummary: Meredydd AP YR Arglwydd родился около 1182 г. в местности мачэн, монмоутшир, Уэльс. Его отца звали Yr Arglwydd Ap Rhys. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын от Gwerfil Ap Morgan.

es.lifeSummary: Meredydd AP YR Arglwydd nació aproximadamente 1182, en Machen, Monmouthshire, Gales como hijo de Yr Arglwydd Ap Rhys. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Gwerfil Ap Morgan.
